title: ATTICUS Hype-Capability-Control upgrade baseline
content: ATTICUS has a new deterministic Hype-Capability-Control detection layer intended to classify AI and tech claims into HYPE, CAPABILITY, CONTROL, or UNKNOWN. The layer is positioned after ATFP signal refinement and before W-Stack reasoning, and is designed to separate narrative inflation from real technical performance and gatekeeping dynamics. The upgrade includes a compact scoring model using evidence strength, environment realism, access restriction, fear narrative, and incentive pressure. A starter 20-row calibration dataset was created from a video transcript analyzing frontier-model hype narratives, with artifact-ready JSONL, CSV, and JSON schema forms. Recommended implementation path is a lightweight Python module with auditability and later balanced expansion using counterexamples from official claims and third-party validation.
context_summary: Introduces a deterministic Hype-Capability-Control layer plus seed calibration dataset for ATTICUS narrative-vs-capability analysis.
